title: MBLEx D2 Kinesiology v2 (applied, massage-relevant best-answer)

questions.prompt: During massage intake, a client shows forward head and upper cervical compression. Which muscle group is most likely short and needs gentle lengthening?
questions.why: Forward-head posture often shortens suboccipitals, increasing upper cervical compression during static sitting.
questions.trap: SCM may feel tight too, but suboccipitals are the most direct upper-cervical shortness finding.

questions.prompt: During postural observation, rounded shoulders and protracted scapulae are present. Which muscle is most likely short and overactive?
questions.why: Short pectoralis minor drives scapular anterior tilt and protraction in common desk-posture patterns.
questions.trap: Rhomboids are often lengthened under load here, not the primary short driver.

questions.prompt: During a wall push-up screen, the medial scapular border lifts away from the rib cage. Which muscle is most likely underactive?
questions.why: Serratus anterior helps anchor and protract the scapula against the thorax during pushing.
questions.trap: Levator scapulae elevates the scapula but does not prevent classic winging.

questions.prompt: A client demonstrates pelvic drop on the swing side during single-leg stance. Which muscle is most likely weak on the stance side?
questions.why: Stance-side gluteus medius is essential for frontal-plane pelvic control during single-leg loading.
questions.trap: Adductors contribute to hip control but are not the primary anti-drop stabilizers.
questions.hook: Hip drop points to glute med.

questions.prompt: A clear foot slap appears right after heel strike. Which muscle is most likely underperforming eccentrically?
questions.why: Tibialis anterior controls plantarflexion after contact to lower the forefoot smoothly.
questions.trap: Gastrocnemius contributes to push-off, not controlled forefoot lowering after heel strike.

questions.prompt: A client elevates the shoulder early during painful overhead reach. Which muscle is most likely overactive in this compensation?
questions.why: Excessive early shrugging often reflects upper trapezius dominance over coordinated scapular rotation.
questions.trap: Lower trapezius usually needs support in this pattern, not downregulation first.

questions.prompt: In standing, anterior pelvic tilt is paired with lumbar extension bias. Which muscle is commonly short and overactive?
questions.why: Iliopsoas shortness commonly contributes to anterior pelvic pull and extension-biased posture.
questions.trap: Gluteus maximus is often lengthened or underactive in this presentation.

questions.prompt: During gait loading response, normal pronation is most useful for which purpose?
questions.why: Early stance pronation helps absorb load and adapt the foot to the surface.
questions.trap: Rigid locking is more relevant later in stance near propulsion.

questions.prompt: During a bodyweight squat, the primary movement plane at the hips and knees is:
questions.why: Squatting is dominated by flexion and extension, which occur in the sagittal plane.
questions.trap: Other planes contribute stability, but they are not the primary movement planes.

questions.prompt: During a jumping jack, shoulder and hip abduction-adduction occur primarily in which plane?
questions.why: Abduction and adduction are classically frontal-plane motions around an anterior-posterior axis.
questions.trap: Transverse rotation can occur, but it is not the dominant action here.

questions.prompt: A driver rotates the trunk to check a blind spot. This action is primarily in which plane?
questions.why: Axial trunk rotation is a transverse-plane movement used during blind-spot checking.
questions.trap: Extension may accompany turning, but rotation remains the primary movement.

questions.prompt: For smooth overhead shoulder motion, which muscle pair most supports scapular upward rotation?
questions.why: Serratus anterior and lower trapezius form a key force-couple for efficient upward rotation.
questions.trap: Downward rotator dominance usually limits comfortable overhead range.

questions.prompt: During massage assessment of neck pain with elevated scapula, which muscle is a high-value palpation target for overactivity?
questions.why: Levator scapulae often contributes to elevated scapular posture and cervicoscapular tension patterns.
questions.trap: Lower trapezius is typically under-recruited in this presentation, not overactive.

questions.prompt: A desk worker shows scapular protraction and anterior tilt. Which muscle should usually be prioritized for relaxation or lengthening?
questions.why: Pectoralis minor shortness strongly drives anterior tilt and protracted scapular resting position.
questions.trap: Rhomboids may ache, but often from lengthened overload rather than short dominance.
questions.hook: Protraction often starts at pec minor.

questions.prompt: A client cannot hinge without lumbar extension gripping. Which tissue often benefits from downregulating tone before movement cueing?
questions.why: Overactive thoracolumbar extensors can dominate hinge attempts and limit segmental load sharing.
questions.trap: Core cueing helps, but persistent extensor gripping often needs direct tone reduction first.

questions.prompt: During squat descent, knees collapse inward repeatedly. Which muscle group is most likely underactive proximally?
questions.why: Weak lateral hip control commonly permits dynamic valgus under descending load.
questions.trap: Quadriceps contribute knee control, but they do not primarily stop femoral internal collapse.

questions.prompt: A persistent toe-out gait is paired with limited hip internal rotation. Which structure is most likely short?
questions.why: Restricted hip internal rotation with toe-out often reflects short or dominant lateral rotators.
questions.trap: Adductor tension can affect gait, but it is less specific to this rotation pattern.

questions.prompt: Nighttime numbness in thumb-index-middle fingers worsens with sustained wrist flexion. Which structure is most relevant?
questions.why: Median distribution with wrist-flexion provocation strongly points toward carpal tunnel involvement.
questions.trap: Cubital tunnel patterns typically emphasize ring and little finger symptoms.
questions.hook: Median night symptoms, think carpal tunnel.

questions.prompt: Ring and little finger paresthesia appears during long elbow-flexed phone use. Which site is most relevant?
questions.why: Ulnar-distribution paresthesia with prolonged elbow flexion fits cubital tunnel irritation best.
questions.trap: Carpal tunnel can cause hand numbness but usually in median nerve digits.

questions.prompt: Painful shoulder elevation is paired with scapular anterior tilt at rest. Which short muscle is most likely involved?
questions.why: Pectoralis minor shortness promotes anterior tilt and can reduce comfortable overhead mechanics.
questions.trap: Serratus anterior weakness matters, but short pec minor often drives resting tilt bias.
questions.hook: Anterior tilt often means pec minor.

questions.prompt: A resting downwardly rotated scapula limits overhead comfort. Which overactive pair is most likely contributing?
questions.why: Downward rotator dominance commonly comes from levator and rhomboid overactivity.
questions.trap: Lower trapezius and serratus are usually the underperforming upward rotators in this pattern.

questions.prompt: A client stands with knees locked back into hyperextension. Which muscle group often lacks enough supportive control?
questions.why: Insufficient hamstring control can allow persistent recurvatum and posterior knee loading.
questions.trap: Quadriceps dominance may coexist, but hamstring support is a frequent missing element.

questions.prompt: During terminal stance, the foot fails to resupinate and stays collapsed medially. Which muscle is commonly underactive?
questions.why: Tibialis posterior helps support medial arch and resupination for efficient propulsion.
questions.trap: Peroneals can be active laterally, but they do not restore medial arch control alone.

questions.prompt: Ankle dorsiflexion is limited with knee straight but improves when knee bends. Which muscle is likely short?
questions.why: Knee-flexed improvement suggests a two-joint plantarflexor restriction, pointing to gastrocnemius.
questions.trap: Soleus tightness limits dorsiflexion in both knee positions more consistently.

questions.prompt: Ankle dorsiflexion remains restricted with both knee-bent and knee-straight testing. Which muscle is most likely short?
questions.why: Persistent restriction across knee positions suggests soleus contribution to dorsiflexion limitation.
questions.trap: Gastrocnemius-driven limits typically improve when the knee is flexed.

questions.prompt: Posterior thigh tension appears in straight-leg raise, but neural sensitizers are negative. Most likely source?
questions.why: When neural provocation is clear, muscular extensibility limits are the more likely driver.
questions.trap: Symptom location alone can overcall nerve involvement without supportive test behavior.

questions.prompt: A thoracic-kyphotic desk client has neck pain during massage. Which region should be broadly relaxed before detailed neck work?
questions.why: Anterior chest stiffness reinforces protraction and forward-head mechanics that load the neck.
questions.trap: Local neck treatment helps symptoms, but missing chest restriction often limits carryover.

questions.prompt: A client overuses upper trapezius during arm elevation. Which muscle often needs support after tone-reduction work?
questions.why: Lower trapezius assists balanced scapular rotation and reduces shrug-dominant compensation.
questions.trap: Latissimus dominance can further bias downward rotation and worsen elevation quality.
questions.hook: Calm upper trap, support lower trap.

questions.prompt: Medial scapular border ache appears after long desk sessions with protraction. Which tissue is often lengthened and overloaded?
questions.why: Rhomboids can become irritated from prolonged lengthened load during protracted posture.
questions.trap: Pectoralis minor is often short here, but not the lengthened medial-border overload tissue.

questions.prompt: During gait, contralateral pelvic drop in mid-stance most strongly indicates deficit in which muscle group?
questions.why: Mid-stance pelvic leveling depends heavily on stance-side abductor control.
questions.trap: Plantarflexors influence propulsion timing but do not primarily maintain pelvic level.

questions.prompt: A client has early heel rise with compensatory pronation and tight calves. Which mechanical restriction is most central?
questions.why: Insufficient dorsiflexion often forces early heel rise and downstream pronation compensation.
questions.trap: Knee extension limits can coexist but are less specific to this combined gait pattern.
questions.hook: Early rise often means limited dorsiflexion.

questions.prompt: A slumped sitting posture shows posterior pelvic tilt and flattened lumbar curve. Which tissue is commonly short?
questions.why: Short hamstrings can pull the pelvis posteriorly and flatten lumbar lordosis in sitting.
questions.trap: Hip flexors are often lengthened in this specific slumped position pattern.

questions.prompt: During serratus testing in overhead reach, scapular winging persists. Which palpation location is most relevant?
questions.why: Serratus fibers along the lateral ribs are key for scapular adherence and upward rotation.
questions.trap: Posterior shoulder palpation alone misses the primary scapulothoracic driver here.

questions.prompt: Lateral hip tenderness with pelvic drop and internal femoral collapse often reflects overload in which compensator?
questions.why: TFL often overworks when lateral hip stability strategy is inefficient under single-leg demand.
questions.trap: Vastus medialis supports patellar tracking but is not the main lateral hip compensator.

questions.prompt: Posterior shoulder stiffness limits internal rotation in an overhead pattern. Which palpation target is most relevant?
questions.why: Posterior cuff stiffness, especially infraspinatus, can limit internal rotation quality overhead.
questions.trap: Anterior chest release can help posture, but this specific restriction is posterior cuff dominant.

questions.prompt: During shoulder abduction in midrange, efficient scapulohumeral rhythm is closest to which ratio?

choices.B: Three to one scapular to glenohumeral
choices.D: Two to one glenohumeral to scapular movement

questions.answer: Two to one glenohumeral to scapular movement
questions.why: A roughly 2:1 glenohumeral-to-scapular contribution supports efficient, less-compensated elevation.
questions.trap: Exact values vary, but 1:1 is usually not the expected midrange pattern.

questions.prompt: In early shoulder abduction around the first 30 degrees, which segment usually contributes most movement?

choices.A: Glenohumeral joint with minimal scapular motion

questions.answer: Glenohumeral joint with minimal scapular motion
questions.why: Early abduction is often glenohumeral-dominant before larger scapular rotation contribution begins.
questions.trap: Scapular motion matters early, but not usually as the dominant first contributor.

questions.prompt: During loading response in gait, pronation should usually be interpreted as which finding?

choices.A: Always pathological and to be eliminated
choices.B: A normal load-management mechanism when controlled
choices.C: A sign of immediate plantarflexor rupture

questions.answer: A normal load-management mechanism when controlled
questions.why: Controlled pronation is a normal shock-management strategy during early stance loading.
questions.trap: Overpronation can matter clinically, but pronation itself is not automatically pathological.

questions.prompt: A massage client presents with forward head, rounded shoulders, and painful overhead arc. Which tissue should often be prioritized first to reduce anterior scapular tilt?
questions.why: Pectoralis minor frequently drives anterior tilt and protraction that narrow comfortable overhead mechanics.
questions.trap: Upper trapezius may be symptomatic, but pec minor shortness often sets the scapular starting fault.
questions.hook: Painful arc? start at pec minor.

questions.prompt: Step-down shows knee valgus plus contralateral pelvic drop. Which proximal deficit most strongly links both findings?
questions.why: Gluteus medius deficit can permit pelvic drop and femoral internal collapse simultaneously.
questions.trap: Quadriceps influence knee extension, but they do not primarily control this frontal-plane chain.

questions.prompt: An overhead worker reports hand paresthesia that eases when the arm drops. Which contributor is most relevant for manual downregulation first?

choices.A: Pectoralis minor and scalene tension pattern

questions.answer: Pectoralis minor and scalene tension pattern
questions.why: Thoracic-outlet style provocation often involves chest and scalene tension with overhead postures.
questions.trap: Distal hand symptoms can distract, but proximal outlet mechanics may be the key driver.

questions.prompt: Foot pronation with tibial internal rotation and medial knee strain persists in gait. Which muscle is most strategic to assess for underactivity?
questions.why: Tibialis posterior supports medial arch control and helps resist excessive collapse patterns.
questions.trap: Peroneus longus supports foot mechanics too, but is less central for this medial-control deficit.
questions.hook: Medial collapse often needs tib posterior.

questions.prompt: A client leans trunk over the stance leg during painful gait. The main biomechanical effect is:

choices.B: Increased hamstring length in terminal swing
choices.C: Reduced demand on stance-side hip abductors

questions.answer: Reduced demand on stance-side hip abductors
questions.why: Lateral trunk lean shifts center of mass to reduce hip abductor moment requirements.
questions.trap: This can be protective short term, but often indicates unresolved lateral hip control issues.

questions.prompt: Scapular winging persists during pushing, and neck tension spikes during manual work. Which muscle is commonly overworking as a compensator?
questions.why: When serratus control is poor, upper trapezius often compensates and increases cervicoscapular strain.
questions.trap: Lower trapezius usually needs support rather than additional inhibition in this scenario.

questions.prompt: Asymmetric lumbar side-bend restriction with ipsilateral flank tone suggests hypertonicity in which muscle?
questions.why: Quadratus lumborum strongly influences side-bending asymmetry and flank tension patterns.
questions.trap: Lumbar erectors matter, but focal side-bend asymmetry often implicates quadratus first.

questions.prompt: Posterior hip-to-thigh symptoms worsen with prolonged sitting and hip external rotation, while lumbar repeated motions are clear. Most likely source?
questions.why: Hip-position provocation with clear lumbar repeated motion supports a deep gluteal neural interface source.
questions.trap: Location alone can mimic lumbar referral, so movement behavior is the decisive clue.

questions.prompt: Painful shoulder abduction occurs with resting scapular downward rotation. Which overactive pair should often be softened before motor retraining?
questions.why: Downward-rotation dominance from levator-rhomboid tone can impair efficient upward rotation during elevation.
questions.trap: Lower trapezius and serratus are typically the pair to facilitate, not downregulate first.

questions.prompt: Foot slap with exaggerated high-stepping in swing is most consistent with which primary deficit?
questions.why: High stepping often compensates for weak dorsiflexion needed to clear toes during swing.
questions.trap: Plantarflexor stiffness can contribute, but this pattern points first to dorsiflexor control.

questions.prompt: Anterior pelvic tilt and rib flare increase lumbar compression sensitivity during prone work. Which tissue group should often be downregulated first?
questions.why: Short or dominant hip flexors can reinforce anterior pelvic pull and extension bias.
questions.trap: Hamstrings may feel tight, but they are less likely to drive this anterior tilt profile.
questions.hook: Rib flare plus tilt: calm hip flexors.

questions.prompt: If shoulder elevation shows excessive early scapular elevation with limited posterior tilt, which muscle is commonly dominant?
questions.why: Early shrug-dominant elevation commonly reflects upper trapezius dominance over coordinated upward rotation.
questions.trap: Serratus weakness may coexist, but early elevation dominance itself signals upper-trap overdrive.
